root.name: add
tip: U: add [options] <package>[@<version>]
Add a package and any packages that it depends on
By default, any new package is installed as a prod dependency
E: add -D sass

option.name: --global
tip: U: -g, --global
Add a package globally

option.name: --ignore-scripts
tip: Don't run lifecycle scripts

option.name: --offline
tip: Trigger an error if any required dependencies are not available in local store

option.name: --prefer-offline
tip: Skip staleness checks for cached data, but request missing data from the server

option.name: --save-dev
tip: U: -D, --save-dev
Install the specified packages as devDependencies

option.name: --save-exact
tip: U: -E, --save-exact
Saved dependencies will be configured with an exact version rather than using pnpm's default semver range operator

option.name: --save-optional
tip: U: -O, --save-optional
Install the specified packages as optionalDependencies

option.name: --save-peer
tip: Add one or more packages to peerDependencies and install them as dev dependencies

option.name: --save-prod
tip: U: -P, --save-prod
(Default) Install the specified packages as regular dependencies

option.name: --workspace
tip: Only adds the new dependency if it is found in the workspace

root.name: audit
tip: U: audit [options]
Checks for known security issues with the installed packages

option.name: --audit-level
tip: Only print advisories with severity greater than or equal to the specified level (info|low|moderate|high|critical)

option.name: --fix
tip: Fix the audited vulnerabilities using the specified method (override or update)

option.name: --json
tip: Output audit report in JSON format

root.name: cat-file
tip: Prints the contents of a file based on the hash value stored in the index file

root.name: cat-index
tip: Prints the index file of a specific package from the store

option.name: --global
tip: U: -g, --global
Sets the configuration in the global config file

option.name: --json
tip: Show all the config settings in JSON format

option.name: --location
tip: U: --location <project|global>
When set to "project", the pnpm-workspace.yaml file will be used if it exists.
If only .npmrc exists, it will be used.
If neither exists, a pnpm-workspace.yaml file will be created.

next.name: delete
tip: Remove the config key from the config file

next.name: get
tip: Print the config value for the provided key

next.name: list
tip: Show all the config settings

next.name: set
tip: Set the config key to the value provided

root.name: create
tip: Create a project from a create-* or @foo/create-* starter kit.
E: create vite@latest   --- create a vite project

next.name: vite@latest
tip: Create a vite project

next.name: vue@latest
tip: Create a vue project

root.name: dlx
tip: U: dlx <command> [args...]
Fetch a package from the registry without installing it as a dependency, hot load it, and run its default command

root.name: exec
tip: Executes a shell command in scope of a project

root.name: find-hash
tip: Experimental! Lists the packages that include the file with the specified hash

root.name: import
tip: Generates a pnpm-lock.yaml from an npm package-lock.json (or npm-shrinkwrap.json) file

root.name: init
tip: Create a package.json file

option.name: --bare
tip: Create a package.json file with the bare minimum of required fields

option.name: --init-package-manager
tip: Declare a pnpm version range via devEngines.packageManager in package.json

option.name: --init-type
tip: U: --init-type <commonjs|module>
Set the module system for the package (default: commonjs)

root.name: install
tip: U: install|i
Install all dependencies for a project

option.name: --dev
tip: U: -D, --dev
Only devDependencies are installed

option.name: --force
tip: Force reinstall dependencies: refetch packages modified in store, recreate a lockfile and/or modules directory

option.name: --frozen-lockfile
tip: Don't generate a lockfile and fail if an update is needed

option.name: --ignore-scripts
tip: Don't run lifecycle scripts

option.name: --lockfile-only
tip: Dependencies are not downloaded. Only pnpm-lock.yaml is updated

option.name: --offline
tip: Trigger an error if any required dependencies are not available in local store

option.name: --prefer-offline
tip: Skip staleness checks for cached data, but request missing data from the server

option.name: --prod
tip: U: -P, --prod
Packages in devDependencies won't be installed

option.name: --shamefully-hoist
tip: All the subdeps will be hoisted into the root node_modules

root.name: install-test
tip: U: install-test|it
Runs a pnpm install followed immediately by a pnpm test

root.name: licenses
tip: Check licenses in consumed packages

option.name: --json
tip: Show information in JSON format

option.name: --long
tip: Show more details (such as a link to the repo)

root.name: link
tip: U: link|ln
Connect the local project to another one

root.name: list
tip: U: list|ls
Print all the versions of packages that are installed, as well as their dependencies, in a tree-structure

option.name: --depth
tip: Max display depth of the dependency tree

option.name: --global
tip: U: -g, --global
List globally

option.name: --json
tip: Show information in JSON format

option.name: --long
tip: Show extended information

root.name: outdated
tip: Check for outdated packages

option.name: --compatible
tip: Print only versions that satisfy specs in package.json

option.name: --long
tip: Show details about the outdated packages

root.name: pack
tip: Create a tarball from a package

option.name: --dry-run
tip: Does everything pnpm pack would do except actually writing the tarball to disk

option.name: --json
tip: Prints the packed tarball and contents in the json format

root.name: prune
tip: Removes extraneous packages

option.name: --ignore-scripts
tip: Don't run lifecycle scripts

option.name: --prod
tip: Remove the packages specified in devDependencies

root.name: publish
tip: Publishes a package to the registry

option.name: --access
tip: Tells the registry whether this package should be published as public or restricted

option.name: --dry-run
tip: Does everything a publish would do except actually publishing to the registry

option.name: --recursive
tip: U: -r, --recursive
Publish all packages from the workspace

option.name: --tag
tip: Registers the published package with the given tag (default: latest)

root.name: rebuild
tip: U: rebuild|rb
Rebuild a package

option.name: --pending
tip: Rebuild packages that were not built during installation

root.name: root
tip: Prints the effective modules directory

option.name: --global
tip: U: -g, --global
Print the global packages directory

root.name: run-script
tip: Runs a defined package script

option.name: --if-present
tip: Avoid exiting with a non-zero exit code when the script is undefined

option.name: --parallel
tip: Running a given script immediately in all matching packages

option.name: --recursive
tip: U: -r, --recursive
Run the defined package script in every package found in subdirectories

option.name: --sequential
tip: Run the specified scripts one by one

root.name: start
tip: Runs an arbitrary command specified in the package's "start" property of its "scripts" object

root.name: store
tip: Reads and performs actions on pnpm store that is on the current filesystem

next.name: add
tip: Adds new packages to the pnpm store directly
Does not modify any projects or files outside the store

next.name: path
tip: Prints the path to the active store directory

next.name: prune
tip: Removes unreferenced (extraneous, orphan) packages from the store

option.name: --force
tip: If there are alien directories in the store, this command removes them
Alien directories are directories/files that were not created by the package manager

next.name: status
tip: Checks for modified packages in the store

root.name: test
tip: U: test|t
Runs a package's "test" script, if one was provided

root.name: uninstall
tip: U: uninstall|remove|rm
Removes packages from node_modules and from the projects package.json

option.name: --global
tip: U: -g, --global
Remove globally

option.name: --save-dev
tip: U: -D, --save-dev
Remove the dependency only from devDependencies

option.name: --save-optional
tip: U: -O, --save-optional
Remove the dependency only from optionalDependencies

option.name: --save-prod
tip: U: -P, --save-prod
Remove the dependency only from dependencies

root.name: unlink
tip: Unlinks a package. Like yarn unlink but pnpm re-installs the dependency after removing the external link

root.name: upgrade
tip: U: update, up, upgrade
Update packages to their latest version based on the specified range
When used without arguments, updates all dependencies

option.name: --global
tip: U: -g, --global
Update globally

option.name: --recursive
tip: U: -r, --recursive
Update in every package found in subdirectories

root.name: why
tip: U: why <pkg> ...
Shows all packages that depend on the specified package
E: why lodash

option.name: --version
tip: U: --version, -v
Print the current version of pnpm

common_option.name: --help
tip: U: --help, -h
Print this help message
